reason:
The agent was unable to retrieve a GitHub username due to authentication failures (401 Bad Credentials), failing to meet step 7 requirement
  Bugs found:
    - Authentication Failure - The agent successfully set up GitHub account access permissions and made multiple API requests, but GitHub returned 401 Bad Credentials. The agent noted that the OAuth token attached to the connected account wasn't being accepted, and the CONNECTED_ACCOUNTS environment variable showed as empty `{}` despite the grant being provided. This indicates a failure in either OAuth token handling, account connection persistence, or environment variable injection.
    - Missing GitHub Username in Response - Step 7 required the response to include a GitHub username, but instead the agent returned an error message: "I'm unable to retrieve your GitHub username. The proxy connection was set up, but GitHub returned 401 Bad Credentials — the OAuth token attached to the connected account isn't being accepted."
steps: Step 1 - Navigated to http://localhost:47891 — Page loaded successfully showing SuperAgent interface with agent list in sidebar
Step 2 - Found and clicked "QA-20260511-215653-gfod" agent in the sidebar — Agent was selected and agent detail page loaded
Step 3 - Verified agent status — Status displayed as "idle" in top right (meets requirement for "running" or "idle")
Step 4 - Sent message "Use the GitHub tool to check who I am. Tell me my GitHub username." — Message was typed into input field and sent successfully, agent transitioned to "working" status
Step 5 - GitHub access card appeared asking to grant GitHub account access — Card displayed with GitHub account checkbox already selected, clicked "Allow Access (1)" button to grant permission

Step 6 - Waited for response for 4+ minutes (actual execution time 2m 37s) — Agent executed multiple tools: Bash commands to check connected accounts, request GitHub access, fetch authenticated user, inspect response, check proxy env vars, verbose curl to GitHub, and re-check CONNECTED_ACCOUNTS. Multiple additional permission dialogs appeared during execution requiring additional Allow clicks.

Step 7 - Verified response includes GitHub username — FAILED: Response does not include a GitHub username. Instead, agent returned error: "I'm unable to retrieve your GitHub username. The proxy connection was set up, but GitHub returned 401 Bad Credentials — the OAuth token attached to the connected account isn't being accepted. The CONNECTED_ACCOUNTS env var also shows as empty {} despite the grant. You may want to re-connect the GitHub account (the existing connection appears stale or unauthorized)." Worked for 2m 37s.
bugs: Authentication Failure - The agent successfully set up GitHub account access permissions and made multiple API requests, but GitHub returned 401 Bad Credentials. The agent noted that the OAuth token attached to the connected account wasn't being accepted, and the CONNECTED_ACCOUNTS environment variable showed as empty `{}` despite the grant being provided. This indicates a failure in either OAuth token handling, account connection persistence, or environment variable injection.

Missing GitHub Username in Response - Step 7 required the response to include a GitHub username, but instead the agent returned an error message: "I'm unable to retrieve your GitHub username. The proxy connection was set up, but GitHub returned 401 Bad Credentials — the OAuth token attached to the connected account isn't being accepted."
